meta.name: BBQ Ribs (Low-and-Slow, Smoker or Oven Finish)

source.name: SSA Canonical Catalog
notes: Classic BBQ ribs with a balanced dry rub and optional sauce set.
Written for SSA planners/adapters: clear equipment, timers, doneness cues, and method variations.

yield.notes: Assumes ~1 rack (baby back) feeds 2–3 people depending on sides. Spare ribs are meatier: plan accordingly.

ingredients.label: Pork ribs (baby back or St. Louis spare ribs)
ingredients.notes: Baby back cooks faster; spare ribs are richer and slightly longer cook. Remove membrane for best bite.

ingredients.label: Yellow mustard (binder, optional)
ingredients.notes: Thin layer to help rub adhere; flavor cooks off.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: Adjust if rub blend already contains salt. For spare ribs, you may increase slightly.

ingredients.label: Brown sugar (light or dark)
ingredients.notes: Helps bark and caramelization. For hot-and-fast ribs reduce sugar to avoid burning.

ingredients.label: Chili powder (mild)
ingredients.notes: Use more for a deeper chili backbone; keep mild for family-friendly.

ingredients.label: Ground cumin (optional)
ingredients.notes: Optional smoky-earthy note; not required for classic ribs.

ingredients.label: Apple juice (spritz / wrap moisture)
ingredients.notes: Use in a spray bottle or add a splash in the wrap. Can substitute apple cider vinegar + water.

ingredients.label: Apple cider vinegar (spritz option)
ingredients.notes: Mix 1:1 with water for spritz. Helps balance sweetness and keep surface moist.

ingredients.label: Butter (wrap option, optional)
ingredients.notes: Classic 3-2-1 style uses butter in the wrap for richness; optional.

ingredients.label: Honey (wrap option, optional)
ingredients.notes: Optional sweetness in wrap; omit for less-sweet BBQ.

ingredients.label: BBQ sauce (for glaze/set, optional)
ingredients.notes: Use your preferred style (KC tomato-based, mild sweet heat, etc.). Thin with a splash of water if very thick.

ingredients.label: Smoking wood (oak/pecan/apple/cherry)
ingredients.notes: Amount depends on smoker type. Fruit woods are excellent for ribs.

steps.title: Prep ribs and remove membrane
steps.body: Pat ribs dry. Flip bone-side up and remove the thin membrane (silver skin): slide a butter knife under it over a middle bone, lift, then grip with a paper towel and pull off. Trim any loose flap edges.

steps.title: Mix the dry rub
steps.body: Combine kosher salt, brown sugar, paprika, black pepper, garlic powder, onion powder, chili powder, and optional cayenne/cumin. Break up any brown sugar clumps so it distributes evenly.

steps.title: Season ribs
steps.body: Optional: spread a very thin layer of yellow mustard over both sides of the ribs as a binder. Apply dry rub evenly on both sides, pressing to adhere. Let sit 15–30 minutes while the smoker heats (or refrigerate uncovered 2–12 hours for deeper seasoning and better bark).

steps.title: Preheat smoker
steps.body: Preheat smoker to 225–250°F (107–121°C). Add your smoking wood and aim for clean smoke (thin blue). If your smoker uses a water pan, fill it for stability.

steps.title: Smoke to set bark
steps.body: Place ribs on the smoker bone-side down (or per your smoker’s airflow pattern). Smoke at 225–250°F for about 2.5–3 hours, until the rub is set and the surface is a deep red-brown. Optional: spritz lightly every 45–60 minutes after the first 90 minutes, once bark has set.

steps.title: Wrap to tenderize (optional but common)
steps.body: For a classic tender rib: wrap racks tightly in foil (or butcher paper). Add a splash of apple juice and optional butter/honey before sealing. Return wrapped ribs to smoker and cook 1.5–2 hours until ribs are noticeably tender. If you prefer firmer ‘bite-through’ ribs, shorten this phase or skip wrapping and continue unwrapped.

steps.title: Unwrap and set glaze (optional)
steps.body: Carefully unwrap (hot steam). Return ribs to smoker unwrapped. If using BBQ sauce, brush a thin layer on and let it set 15–30 minutes. Keep heat at 225–250°F. Apply a second light coat if desired. Avoid thick, sugary layers that can burn.

steps.title: Check doneness (bend test + bone pullback)
steps.body: Ribs are done when they pass the bend test: lift from the middle with tongs—rack bends easily and the surface may crack slightly. Look for 1/4–1/2 inch bone pullback. Meat should be tender but not falling off the bone unless that’s your preference. Internal temp is less reliable for ribs; texture is key.

steps.title: Rest, slice, and serve
steps.body: Rest ribs 10–15 minutes to let juices settle. Slice between bones with a sharp knife. Serve with classic BBQ sides (slaw, beans, potato salad, cornbread).

steps.title: Storage and reheating
steps.body: Cool leftovers quickly. Refrigerate tightly wrapped up to 4 days; freeze up to 3 months. Reheat covered in a 275°F oven with a splash of water/juice until warmed through; uncover briefly to re-set bark or glaze.

leftovers.notes: Plan ribs as a weekend cook. Pair with storehouse sides; schedule leftover use within 1–2 days.

notes: No smoker: bake wrapped ribs in oven at 275°F for ~2.5–3 hours, then sauce and broil briefly to set.
Pellet grill: maintain stable temp; keep sauce thin and set at the end to avoid scorching.
Aim for bend-test tenderness (bite-through) unless you intentionally want fall-off-the-bone.
Wrapping speeds tenderness but can soften bark; finish unwrapped to re-firm the surface.
